title: Heterocentron hirtellum
intro: Heterocentron hirtellum, commonly known as the hairy Heterocentron or the velvet-leaf, is a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its reported health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Heterocentron hirtellum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is typically prepared as a tea or an infusion, or sometimes made into a tincture, to harness its therapeutic benefits.
taxonomy: Heterocentron hirtellum is classified within the family Melastomataceae, a family of flowering plants commonly known as the melastome or memecyclid family. This family is part of the order Myrtales, which also includes other families such as the Myrtaceae and the Lythraceae. The classification of Heterocentron hirtellum is further defined by its placement within the tribe Heterocentreae, a tribe of flowering plants within the family Melastomataceae. The family Melastomataceae is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of the Americas, Africa, and Asia. The classification of Heterocentron hirtellum reflects its unique characteristics and its evolutionary history within the Melastomataceae family.
